data.title: Church & Dwight exceeded 2025 outlook with $6,203.2 million in net sales (1.6% increase) and 0.7% organic sales growth.
data.description: Ewing, New Jersey-based Church & Dwight Co., Inc. (NYSE: CHD) revealed that it has surpassed its expectations with robust sales and earnings growth, resulting in market share expansion across its worldwide portfolio. The company reported a 1.6% increase in full-year 2025 net sales, reaching $6,203.2 million, outperforming its projected 1.5% growth. Despite a 130 basis points decline from its discontinued VMS business and a slowdown in category growth, organic sales saw a 0.7% rise. This impressive performance showcases the company's resilience and adaptability in the market.

data.title: Brookfield Renewable reported strong 2025 results, with $1.33 billion FFO, a 10% increase year-over-year, and a 5% distribution increase.
data.description: Brookfield Renewable Partners reported financial results for the three and twelve months ended December 31, 2025, in US dollars. They had a net income attributable to unitholders of $410 million for three months and a net loss of $19 million for twelve months. Funds From Operations (FFO) for twelve months were $1,334 million, $2.01 per unit, up 10% year-over-year. They had strong operating performance, with hydro segment FFO at $607 million, a 19% YoY increase, and wind/solar segment FFO at $648 million. Brookfield committed $8.8 billion across strategic technologies in core markets and completed $4.5 billion in asset sales. They delivered a record ~8,000 megawatts of new capacity in 2025 globally and expect a run-rate of ~10,000 megawatts per year by 2027. They strengthened their balance sheet with $4.6 billion liquidity. Quarterly distribution was $0.392 per LP unit, payable on March 31, 2026, marking a 5% increase. Brookfield Renewable operates a large renewable power platform with hydroelectric, wind, solar, and storage facilities, and investments in sustainable solutions assets. They expect to deploy capital into growth opportunities and maintain BBB+ investment grade rating. More detailed financial statements are available on their website.

data.title: QuantumScape adds Geoff Ribar to board, a tech industry vet with CFO experience, joining as a director.
data.description: In California, QuantumScape Corporation (NASDAQ: QS), a prominent player in solid-state lithium-metal battery technology, has appointed Geoff Ribar to its board of directors. With a strong background in the technology sector, Ribar has served as CFO for various companies. Notably, he held the CFO position at Cadence Design Systems from 2010 to 2017. His appointment brings a wealth of experience to QuantumScape as they continue to advance in the industry.

data.title: Autoliv's Q4 2025 financial report: $2.8B net sales, 7.7% increase, 11.3% operating margin, $2.98 diluted EPS.
data.description: In the final quarter of 2025, (NYSE: ALV) and (SSE: ALIV.sdb) reported record-breaking financial performance with significant growth across key metrics. Net sales for the period reached $2,817 million, reflecting a 7.7% increase compared to the previous quarter. The company also achieved a strong 4.2% organic sales growth. Operating margin stood at an impressive 11.3%, with an adjusted operating margin of 12.0%. Diluted earnings per share (EPS) were reported at $2.98, showcasing a 4% rise from the previous comparable period. This marked an exceptional quarter for the company, demonstrating robust financial health and performance.
